events.title: Kimi K3 ships
events.detail: Moonshot's 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model lands — the first open '3T-class' model, trailing only Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 on overall capability. China closes the frontier gap.

events.title: GPT-5.6 opens to the public
events.detail: After a two-week federal review, OpenAI clears GPT-5.6 (Sol, Terra, Luna) for everyone — shipped alongside ChatGPT Work, an agent built to carry out whole jobs. The gate lifts.

events.title: Grok 4.5 ships
events.detail: xAI's V9 coding model lands as Grok 4.5 — 1.5T parameters trained on Cursor data, priced at $2 / $6 per Mtok. Still not the fabled 6T Grok 5, which keeps slipping.

events.title: Claude Sonnet 5 ships
events.detail: Anthropic's most agentic Sonnet becomes the default model — near-Opus performance at Sonnet speed and price, aimed squarely at running agents cheaply.

events.title: GPT-5.6 previews under federal gate
events.detail: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 — Sol, Terra and Luna — but access is limited to ~20 US-government-vetted partners pending a pre-release review: the frontier's first regulatory chokepoint.

events.title: Claude Fable 5 ships
events.detail: Anthropic opens the Claude 5 family: always-on adaptive thinking and a 1M-token context window.

events.title: GPT-5.5 ships
events.detail: OpenAI's flagship update lands in ChatGPT and Codex; API access follows a day later.

events.title: Claude Opus 4.5 ships
events.detail: Anthropic's strongest coding model to date lands days after Gemini 3 — the frontier cadence compresses to weeks.

events.title: Gemini 3 ships
events.detail: Google's frontier line jumps again; agentic coding and long-horizon tasks improve another step.

events.title: GPT-5.1 ships
events.detail: OpenAI iterates the GPT-5 line with adaptive reasoning effort and faster default paths.

events.title: GPT-5 ships
events.detail: OpenAI unifies reasoning and fast paths behind one router; frontier access goes mass-market.

events.title: Claude 4 ships
events.detail: Anthropic's Opus 4 and Sonnet 4 push sustained autonomous coding into production use.

events.title: Stargate announced: $500B
events.detail: SoftBank, OpenAI, Oracle and MGX commit $500B over four years — $100B deploying immediately — for US AI datacenters.

events.title: DeepSeek-R1 open weights
events.detail: Frontier-class reasoning released under MIT license at a fraction of incumbent training cost.

events.title: OpenAI o1: the reasoning era
events.detail: Test-time compute becomes the new scaling axis — models that think before they answer.

events.title: GPT-4o: omni goes realtime
events.detail: Native audio-vision-text at half the price of GPT-4 Turbo. Frontier intelligence keeps getting cheaper.

events.title: Claude 3 ships
events.detail: Opus matches GPT-4; for the first time the frontier is contested by more than one lab.

events.title: The Techno-Optimist Manifesto
events.detail: Marc Andreessen publishes the manifesto; e/acc ideas enter mainstream venture discourse.

events.title: GPT-4 ships
events.detail: The capability jump that reset every timeline. $30 per million input tokens at launch.

events.title: ChatGPT launches
events.detail: 100M users in two months. The acceleration becomes visible to everyone.

events.title: e/acc is born
events.detail: A pseudonymous newsletter by @zestular, @BasedBeffJezos, @bayeslord and co. names the movement: build, accelerate, ascend the Kardashev gradient.
